bash -lc 'tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); cd "$tmpdir"; npm init -y >/dev/null; npm install <repo-root> >/dev/null; node --input-type=module -e "import { activateExtensionForTest, assertRegisteredCommandContract } from '\@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk/testing'\; const activation = await activateExtensionForTest({ manifest: { name: '\consumer-ext'\, version: '\0.1.0'\, entry: '\./index.js'\, priority: 0, capabilities: ['\commands'\] }, activate(api) { api.registerCommand({ name: '\consumer hello'\, action: '\consumer-hello'\, description: '\consumer check'\, run: async () => ({ ok: true }) }); } }); const result = assertRegisteredCommandContract(activation.registrations, { command: '\consumer hello'\, action: '\consumer-hello'\, extensionName: '\consumer-ext'\ }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, command: result.command.command, failed: activation.failed.length, warnings: activation.warnings.length }));"'

value.author: sdk-docs-subagent
value.text:
Subagent findings for GitHub #107 afterCommand before/after item state (read-only; no source edits):

Scope/lineage:
- GitHub issue #107 is open and belongs under existing SDK/package platform epic pm-ugqx; do not create a duplicate pm item unless implementation needs a child task at claim time.
- This is distinct from closed pm-e9ut/#104: onWrite now has item-aware before/after snapshots, but afterCommand remains command-level only.

Current surfaces:
- src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts defines BeforeCommandHookContext and AfterCommandHookContext. AfterCommandHookContext currently has ok/error/result only; result is unknown and has no typed affected item state.
- src/sdk/index.ts re-exports AfterCommandHookContext, ItemDocument, ItemFrontMatter, and ItemStatus, so additive type changes there flow to package authors automatically.
- src/core/extensions/extension-hook-runtime.ts clones hook contexts before dispatch; any new payload must stay plain structured data.
- src/cli/main.ts runAndClearAfterCommandHooks constructs the afterCommand event from active command metadata plus getActiveCommandResult(); this is the single command-wrapper insertion point.
- src/core/extensions/index.ts only stores activeCommandResult today. It has no mutation-state accumulator.
- src/cli/register-mutation.ts and src/cli/register-operations.ts call mutate commands then invalidate search and print results. Existing result shapes expose item/changed_fields/ids, but not previous item snapshots.
- src/core/store/item-store.ts mutateItem/deleteItem already has beforeDocument/afterDocument and dispatches them to onWrite for update/delete/history writes.
- src/cli/commands/create.ts has beforeDocument/afterDocument and changedFields for create onWrite events.
- src/cli/commands/restore.ts and src/sdk/package-import-adapters.ts were recently enriched for onWrite and should be mirrored if afterCommand should cover restore/import.
- src/cli/commands/update.ts has local previousStatus for status-specific logic, but the durable source of truth is mutateItem before/after snapshots.

Minimal implementation shape:
- Add optional typed field to AfterCommandHookContext, preferably affected_items?: Array<{ id: string; type?: string; previous_status?: ItemStatus; status?: ItemStatus; before?: ItemDocument; after?: ItemDocument; changed_fields?: string[]; op?: string; path?: string }>.
- Keep result unchanged for compatibility; do not ask package authors to parse unknown result for transition detection.
- Reuse the same item mutation snapshots already produced for onWrite. Best small architecture: add an active per-command mutation accumulator in src/core/extensions/index.ts (for example recordActiveCommandItemMutation/consumeActiveCommandItemMutations) and call it from the same item-write sites that invoke runActiveOnWriteHooks. Then src/cli/main.ts adds affected_items to the afterCommand context.
- Dedupe history/item write pairs so package authors see one logical mutation per item/op, not both update and update:history. Use op without :history as the canonical event and include path if useful.
- For update-many, afterCommand should naturally include all updated items because each runUpdate call records into the active command accumulator. For dry-run/rollback, decide explicitly: dry-run should probably omit affected_items; rollback should include restored before/after where available.
- For close via update auto-route, affected_items should show the final close transition, and if pre-update fields ran first it may either include two entries for the same id or a merged entry. Package-author ergonomics favors merging by id with before from first mutation, after from last mutation, changed_fields union, op such as update+close or close.

Docs to update:
- docs/SDK.md near the hook registration section should document afterCommand affected_items, transition examples, optionality, and the difference from onWrite. Guidance: use afterCommand for command-level notifications and onWrite for low-level file sync.
- docs/EXTENSIONS.md Runtime APIs should mention afterCommand affected_items alongside onWrite fields.
- packages/pm-lifecycle-hooks/README.md and extensions/lifecycle-hooks/index.ts can stay default-inert, or optionally show a commented/README example that checks event.affected_items?.some(item => item.previous_status !== item.status). Keep the package behavior inert.

Tests to add/update:
- tests/unit/extensions-runtime.spec.ts: verify afterCommand enriched context is cloned/isolated and minimal contexts without affected_items still work.
- tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts: compile/import the new AfterCommandHookContext shape from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk with ItemDocument/ItemStatus fields.
- tests/unit/item-store.spec.ts or a new focused unit test: if adding an accumulator, assert mutateItem/deleteItem records logical mutation snapshots without exposing history duplicates.
- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts near existing afterCommand hook tests: install a temp extension that logs event.affected_items for create, update --status, close, delete, and failed get. Assert success commands include before/status/after/status and failed commands omit affected_items while still running with ok=false.
- Add focused coverage for update-many applying status changes to multiple items if accumulator design is used.

Compatibility and UX risks:
- Additive optional field is safe for existing hooks; do not make affected_items required.
- Payload size can grow for large bodies or bulk update-many. Consider including full before/after ItemDocument only for affected_items and documenting that heavy consumers should filter by changed_fields/status. If this becomes too expensive, a lean alternative is previous/current metadata snapshots plus body omitted by default, but issue #107 asks for front-matter snapshot so metadata-only may be enough.
- Naming should be stable and agent-friendly. Prefer snake_case to match pm output fields: affected_items, previous_status, changed_fields.
- Avoid deriving previous_status from command options; command options cannot distinguish reopen/open/unblock correctly. Derive from before.metadata.status and after.metadata.status.
- Failed commands should still run afterCommand with ok=false, but should not contain stale accumulator state; clear/consume state in postAction/finally.
- Extension hooks are cloned via cloneContextSnapshot, so keep documents JSON-serializable and avoid class instances, Dates, Errors, Maps, or functions.

Recommended first implementation slice:
1. Add AfterCommandAffectedItem type and affected_items? to extension-types.ts, exported through SDK barrel automatically.
2. Add active command mutation accumulator helpers in src/core/extensions/index.ts.
3. Record logical item mutations from create, mutateItem, deleteItem, restore, and package import alongside existing onWrite dispatch.
4. Include consumed affected_items in src/cli/main.ts afterCommand context and clear it on no-runtime/failure paths.
5. Document SDK/extension guidance and add the tests above.

value.note: Copyable afterCommand affected transition pattern for package authors

value.author: sdk-ergonomics-subagent
value.text: SDK ergonomics subagent evidence: recommended and applied small package-author slice in packages/pm-lifecycle-hooks/README.md. Added copyable afterCommand event.affected transition filtering pattern so notification/cache packages can use command-level mutation context without parsing result payloads or reading item files. Linked doc path and verification. Verification passed: pnpm build. Note: observed separate in-progress child pm-01bm claimed by codex-sdk-continuation; left untouched.

value.author: coverage-subagent
value.text: Coverage subagent read-only findings (2026-06-06): duplicate check reused existing SDK/package platform epic pm-ugqx; no new pm item created. Current afterCommand affected item context has integration coverage in tests/integration/extension-hooks.integration.spec.ts for create/update/restore/delete and focused smoke coverage passes. Smallest uncovered/fragile patch target is tests/unit/extensions-runtime.spec.ts: add direct unit coverage for recordAfterCommandAffectedItem(), consumeAfterCommandAffectedItems(), and projectAfterCommandItemSnapshot() in src/core/extensions/index.ts. Rationale: src/core/extensions/index.ts is explicitly excluded from coverage thresholds in vitest.config.ts, and existing integration coverage only exercises the accumulator/projector indirectly through CLI commands; branch cases remain fragile: no registered afterCommand hooks should skip recording, consume should clear the accumulator, unset:* changed fields should project the canonical field, omitted heavy fields like body/comments/tests should stay out of afterCommand snapshots, and non-string changed_fields entries should be ignored. Existing checks run by subagent: PM_AUTHOR=coverage-subagent pm context --limit 10; pm search "SDK CLI context management coverage fragile" --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20; pm get pm-ugqx --full; pm get pm-mpbb --full; rg inspections for afterCommand/affected coverage; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/extension-hooks.integration.spec.ts (1 passed); node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions-runtime.spec.ts tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts (38 passed); npm install -g .; pm --version => 2026.6.6; node -v => v25.9.0; pnpm -v => 10.33.4; pnpm build passed. Recommended focused verification after patch: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions-runtime.spec.ts tests/integration/extension-hooks.integration.spec.ts tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts; optional full gate: node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage.

value.author: github-triage-subagent
value.text: Dependency/security triage 2026-06-06: gh Dependabot open alerts returned empty; vulnerability alerts endpoint returned 204 enabled; latest fixed Dependabot alerts are brace-expansion/vite only. pnpm audit --audit-level moderate reported no known vulnerabilities. pnpm security:scan reported no credential-like secrets in tracked files. pnpm outdated --format json reports only commander current 14.0.3 latest 15.0.0 wanted 14.0.3; npm view commander@15.0.0 engines shows node >=22.12.0 while package.json declares node >=20, so do not bump commander unless the package support floor moves past Node 20 or a compatible commander release appears.
